title: Add E2E tests for concurrent CR conflicts and failover
description:
# GitHub Issue #184: Add E2E tests for concurrent CR conflicts and operator pod failover

**Category**: testing | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: Create E2E tests for two untested scenarios: (1) two Keystone CRs in the same namespace sharing database or secret names to verify the secretToKeystoneMapper and reconciler behavior under resource contention, and (2) killing the operator pod during active reconciliation to validate leader election failover and incomplete status update recovery.

**Motivation**: The secretToKeystoneMapper returns reconcile requests for all matching CRs but reconcilers assume exclusive ownership of sub-resources by name. This conflict is untested. Similarly, chaos tests kill dependency pods but never the operator itself, leaving leader election failover and partial-reconcile recovery unvalidated.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`
- `operators/keystone/test/e2e/`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] E2E test deploys two Keystone CRs in the same namespace and verifies no resource conflicts or data corruption
- [ ] E2E test kills operator pod during reconciliation and verifies the new leader resumes correctly
- [ ] Status conditions are consistent after operator pod failover
- [ ] Test verifies no orphaned or duplicate sub-resources after concurrent CR operations

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/184

stories.title: Platform engineer deploys concurrent Keystone CRs with shared dependencies
stories.want: to deploy two Keystone CRs in the same namespace that share the same secretRef and adminPasswordSecretRef names
stories.so_that: I can verify the secretToKeystoneMapper and reconciler handle resource contention without conflicts or data corruption
criteria: Both Keystone CRs reach Ready=True with reason AllReady
Each CR creates its own uniquely-named Deployment ({name}-api), Service, CronJob, ConfigMap, Fernet Secret, Database/User/Grant CRs
The secretToKeystoneMapper enqueues both CRs when the shared Secret changes without causing reconcile errors
No naming collisions occur on sub-resources despite both CRs sharing the same secretRef names

stories.title: Platform engineer verifies resource isolation after deleting one concurrent CR
stories.want: to delete one of two coexisting Keystone CRs and confirm the other is unaffected
stories.so_that: I can safely manage independent Keystone instances sharing infrastructure in the same namespace
criteria: After deleting CR-A, all CR-A owned sub-resources are garbage-collected via owner references
CR-B remains Ready=True with all sub-conditions True after CR-A deletion
CR-B sub-resources (Deployment, Service, CronJob, ConfigMap) remain intact and unchanged
Shared secrets (keystone-db, keystone-admin) remain available after CR-A deletion (ESO-managed, not Keystone-owned)

stories.title: SRE verifies operator resilience to pod failure via leader election failover
stories.want: to kill all operator pods and confirm leader election failover restores reconciliation capability
stories.so_that: I can trust the HA operator deployment (2 replicas with --leader-elect) survives pod failures
criteria: PodChaos with mode: all kills both operator pods in the deployment namespace
Deployment controller restarts killed pods and new leader election occurs
Keystone CR status conditions remain consistent after failover (Ready=True maintained)
Operator liveness and readiness probes pass after recovery

stories.title: SRE verifies new leader reconciles spec changes after failover
stories.want: to patch a Keystone CR after operator pod failover and verify the new leader processes the change
stories.so_that: I can confirm the operator is fully functional after leader election, not just running but actively reconciling
criteria: Patching spec.replicas from 1 to 2 after operator recovery triggers reconciliation by new leader
Deployment replicas update to 2 with availableReplicas >= 2
Ready=True with reason AllReady maintained after the replica patch
DeploymentReady condition reflects the updated replica count

stories.title: CI engineer diagnoses E2E test failures via catch block diagnostics
stories.want: diagnostic output collected automatically when any assert step fails
stories.so_that: I can troubleshoot E2E test failures from CI logs without reproducing the failure locally
criteria: Every assert step in both test suites includes a catch block with diagnostic output
Catch blocks collect Keystone CR status, pod logs (including --previous), and namespace events
Operator pod kill test catch blocks additionally collect operator pod status and Chaos Mesh experiment status
Diagnostics use the shared diagnostics.sh helper where applicable

requirements.description: Two Keystone CRs sharing the same secretRef and adminPasswordSecretRef names in the same namespace SHALL both reach Ready=True
requirements.rationale: The secretToKeystoneMapper enqueues all matching CRs when a shared Secret changes. Both CRs must reconcile independently to Ready=True, proving the mapper correctly routes events and reconcilers handle concurrent invocation.

scenarios.name: Both concurrent CRs reach Ready
scenarios.when: two Keystone CRs (keystone-concurrent-a and keystone-concurrent-b) are applied to the openstack namespace, both referencing secretRef keystone-db and adminPasswordSecretRef keystone-admin
scenarios.then: both CRs eventually have Ready=True with reason AllReady
and_then: all 9 sub-conditions (SecretsReady, FernetKeysReady, CredentialKeysReady, DatabaseReady, DeploymentReady, HPAReady, NetworkPolicyReady, BootstrapReady, TrustFlushReady) are True on both CRs

scenarios.name: Second CR applied while first is reconciling
scenarios.when: keystone-concurrent-b is applied shortly after keystone-concurrent-a (same Chainsaw step)
scenarios.then: the reconciler handles both CRs without errors or resource conflicts
and_then: no error events with reason OwnerRefConflict or AlreadyExists in the namespace

scenarios.name: Shared secret change triggers both CRs
scenarios.when: a shared secret (keystone-db or keystone-admin) is modified
scenarios.then: secretToKeystoneMapper enqueues reconcile requests for both CRs
and_then: both CRs are reconciled and maintain Ready=True

requirements.description: Each Keystone CR SHALL create uniquely-named sub-resources using the CR name as prefix, with no shared sub-resources between concurrent CRs
requirements.rationale: Sub-resources are named {cr-name}-api (Deployment, Service, PDB), {cr-name}-fernet-keys (Secret), {cr-name}-fernet-rotate (CronJob), etc. This naming ensures isolation between CRs in the same namespace.

scenarios.name: Unique Deployments per CR
scenarios.when: both CRs are Ready=True
scenarios.then: Deployment keystone-concurrent-a-api and Deployment keystone-concurrent-b-api both exist with availableReplicas > 0
and_then: each Deployment has app.kubernetes.io/instance matching its own CR name

scenarios.name: Unique Services per CR
scenarios.when: both CRs are Ready=True
scenarios.then: Service keystone-concurrent-a-api and Service keystone-concurrent-b-api both exist with port 5000

scenarios.name: Unique Fernet CronJobs per CR
scenarios.when: both CRs are Ready=True
scenarios.then: CronJob keystone-concurrent-a-fernet-rotate and CronJob keystone-concurrent-b-fernet-rotate both exist

requirements.description: Deleting one Keystone CR SHALL NOT affect the status or sub-resources of the other CR in the same namespace
requirements.rationale: Owner references ensure cascading deletion is scoped to the deleted CR's sub-resources. The remaining CR's sub-resources must not be impacted because they have different owner UIDs.

scenarios.name: Remaining CR stays Ready after sibling deletion
scenarios.when: keystone-concurrent-a is deleted while keystone-concurrent-b is Ready=True
scenarios.then: keystone-concurrent-b remains Ready=True with reason AllReady
and_then: all sub-conditions on keystone-concurrent-b remain True

scenarios.name: Remaining CR resources intact after sibling deletion
scenarios.when: keystone-concurrent-a is deleted
scenarios.then: Deployment keystone-concurrent-b-api, Service keystone-concurrent-b-api, and CronJob keystone-concurrent-b-fernet-rotate still exist

scenarios.name: Shared secrets survive CR deletion
scenarios.when: keystone-concurrent-a is deleted
scenarios.then: Secrets keystone-db and keystone-admin still exist in the namespace
and_then: these secrets are owned by ESO ExternalSecret, not by the Keystone CR

requirements.description: After deleting one concurrent CR, its sub-resources SHALL be garbage-collected with no orphaned or duplicate resources remaining
requirements.rationale: Owner references on all sub-resources enable Kubernetes garbage collection. The test must verify no orphaned Deployments, Services, Jobs, CronJobs, or ConfigMaps remain after CR deletion.

scenarios.name: Deleted CR sub-resources are cleaned up
scenarios.when: keystone-concurrent-a is deleted
scenarios.then: Deployment keystone-concurrent-a-api, Service keystone-concurrent-a-api, and CronJob keystone-concurrent-a-fernet-rotate no longer exist
and_then: error assertion confirms NotFound for each resource

scenarios.name: No duplicate sub-resources remain
scenarios.when: both CRs were Ready and then keystone-concurrent-a is deleted
scenarios.then: exactly one Deployment with label app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone remains (keystone-concurrent-b-api)
and_then: script step counts Deployments matching label and asserts count equals 1

requirements.description: Killing all operator pods via PodChaos SHALL trigger leader election failover with the Deployment controller restarting pods
requirements.rationale: The operator runs 2 replicas with --leader-elect and LeaderElectionID keystone.openstack.c5c3.io. Killing all pods forces the Deployment controller to create new pods, which then compete for the leader lease. This validates the HA deployment model.

scenarios.name: Operator pods recover after kill
scenarios.when: PodChaos with mode: all kills all pods matching app.kubernetes.io/name: keystone-operator
scenarios.then: the Deployment controller restarts the pods
and_then: at least 1 operator pod reaches Ready within the assert timeout

scenarios.name: New leader acquires lease
scenarios.when: all operator pods are killed and restarted
scenarios.then: one of the new pods acquires the leader lease (keystone.openstack.c5c3.io)
and_then: the leader starts processing reconcile events from the work queue

scenarios.name: PodChaos is one-shot
scenarios.when: PodChaos action: pod-kill is applied
scenarios.then: the pods are killed once (no continuous killing)
and_then: the PodChaos CR is deleted before asserting recovery to ensure no ongoing fault injection

requirements.description: Status conditions on the Keystone CR SHALL remain consistent (Ready=True) after operator pod failover
requirements.rationale: The new leader re-reads the CR state from the API server, re-evaluates all sub-conditions, and persists a status update. Since no infrastructure changed (only the operator restarted), all conditions should remain True.

scenarios.name: Ready=True maintained after failover
scenarios.when: the operator pod is killed and a new leader takes over
scenarios.then: the Keystone CR has Ready=True with reason AllReady
and_then: all sub-conditions (SecretsReady, FernetKeysReady, DatabaseReady, DeploymentReady, BootstrapReady) remain True

scenarios.name: No false transitions during failover
scenarios.when: the operator is temporarily unavailable (both pods down)
scenarios.then: status conditions are not modified during the outage window (no controller running to update them)
and_then: after recovery, the conditions are re-evaluated and confirmed True

requirements.description: The new leader SHALL reconcile spec changes (e.g., replica count patch) applied after operator pod failover
requirements.rationale: Leader election only proves lease acquisition. This requirement validates that the new leader's controller watches are active and process mutations, confirming full operational capability post-failover.

scenarios.name: Replica patch applied by new leader
scenarios.when: spec.replicas is patched from 1 to 2 after operator recovery
scenarios.then: the Deployment keystone-chaos-op-api is updated to replicas: 2 with availableReplicas >= 2
and_then: Ready=True maintained after the scaling operation

scenarios.name: Ready condition reflects updated state
scenarios.when: the replica patch triggers a reconciliation cycle
scenarios.then: DeploymentReady=True with reason DeploymentReady after the Deployment reaches desired replicas

requirements.description: Every assert step in both test suites SHALL include a catch block collecting diagnostic output on failure
requirements.rationale: E2E test failures in CI must be debuggable from logs alone. The existing chaos tests (CC-0047) use diagnostics.sh for uniform output. New tests must follow the same pattern for consistent CI diagnostics.

scenarios.name: Catch block on concurrent CR assertions
scenarios.when: any assert step fails in the concurrent-cr-conflicts test
scenarios.then: the catch block collects Keystone CR status for both CRs, all pod logs, and namespace events
and_then: diagnostic output appears in the Chainsaw test output and CI logs

scenarios.name: Catch block on operator pod kill assertions
scenarios.when: any assert step fails in the operator-pod-kill test
scenarios.then: the catch block collects Keystone CR status, operator pod status, Chaos Mesh experiment status, operator logs (including --previous), and namespace events
and_then: diagnostics.sh helper is used for uniform output format

tasks.title: Create concurrent CR conflict E2E test suite: CR fixtures and chainsaw-test with deploy, Ready assertions, sub-resource isolation, deletion, and cleanup verification (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-008)
tasks.description:
Create tests/e2e/keystone/concurrent-cr-conflicts/ with:

**Fixtures:**
- 00-keystone-cr-a.yaml: Keystone CR keystone-concurrent-a, replicas 1, clusterRef openstack-db, database keystone_concurrent_a, secretRef keystone-db, adminPasswordSecretRef keystone-admin
- 01-keystone-cr-b.yaml: Keystone CR keystone-concurrent-b, replicas 1, clusterRef openstack-db, database keystone_concurrent_b, secretRef keystone-db, adminPasswordSecretRef keystone-admin

**chainsaw-test.yaml steps:**
1. Apply both CR fixtures (00-keystone-cr-a.yaml, 01-keystone-cr-b.yaml)
2. Assert both CRs Ready=True with AllReady (all sub-conditions True on each) — catch: dump both CR statuses + pod logs + events
3. Assert unique Deployments: keystone-concurrent-a-api (availableReplicas > 0), keystone-concurrent-b-api (availableReplicas > 0); unique Services: port 5000 each
4. Assert unique Fernet CronJobs: keystone-concurrent-a-fernet-rotate, keystone-concurrent-b-fernet-rotate; unique ConfigMaps: script grep for keystone-concurrent-a-config- and keystone-concurrent-b-config-
5. Delete keystone-concurrent-a; error-assert: Deployment keystone-concurrent-a-api, Service keystone-concurrent-a-api, CronJob keystone-concurrent-a-fernet-rotate all NotFound; script: count Deployments with label app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone, assert count is 1
6. Assert keystone-concurrent-b still Ready=True with AllReady; assert Deployment keystone-concurrent-b-api still has availableReplicas > 0 — catch: dump CR-B status + pod logs + events

**Conventions:** SPDX Apache-2.0 header on all files. Feature ID CC-0066 in comments. Follow basic-deployment test pattern for assertions. timeouts.assert: 5m at spec level. namespace: openstack.

tasks.title: Create operator pod kill chaos E2E test suite: CR fixture, PodChaos manifest, patch file, and chainsaw-test with baseline, kill, recovery, and post-failover reconciliation (REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-007, REQ-008)
tasks.description:
Create tests/e2e-chaos/operator-pod-kill/ with:

**Fixtures:**
- 00-keystone-cr.yaml: Keystone CR keystone-chaos-op, replicas 1, clusterRef openstack-db, database keystone_chaos_op, secretRef keystone-db, adminPasswordSecretRef keystone-admin. Follow existing chaos CR pattern (CC-0047).
- 01-podchaos.yaml: PodChaos kill-operator, action pod-kill, mode all, selector namespaces [default], labelSelectors app.kubernetes.io/name: keystone-operator, gracePeriod 0. mode: all ensures the active leader is killed, forcing leader election.
- 02-patch-replicas.yaml: Keystone patch for keystone-chaos-op, spec.replicas: 2. Used to verify the new leader can reconcile spec changes.

**chainsaw-test.yaml steps:**
1. Apply 00-keystone-cr.yaml
2. Assert baseline Ready=True with AllReady — catch: diagnostics.sh baseline keystone-chaos-op
3. Apply 01-podchaos.yaml (kills all operator pods)
4. Delete PodChaos kill-operator (lift fault, allow Deployment to restart pods cleanly)
5. Assert Keystone CR Ready=True with AllReady (status consistent after failover) — catch: diagnostics.sh chaos keystone-chaos-op --log-label=app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone-operator. Also assert sub-conditions: SecretsReady, FernetKeysReady, DatabaseReady, DeploymentReady, BootstrapReady all True.
6. Patch 02-patch-replicas.yaml (replicas 1 -> 2)
7. Assert Deployment keystone-chaos-op-api has replicas: 2 and availableReplicas >= 2; assert Ready=True maintained — catch: diagnostics.sh chaos keystone-chaos-op --log-label=app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone-operator

**Conventions:** SPDX Apache-2.0 header on all files. Feature ID CC-0066 in comments. Follow mariadb-pod-kill pattern for test structure. timeouts.assert: 5m at spec level. namespace: openstack. PodChaos targets default namespace (operator deployment namespace).

tasks.title: Update docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md with concurrent-cr-conflicts scenario documentation (REQ-008)
tasks.description:
Add the concurrent-cr-conflicts test suite to the existing Keystone E2E test reference documentation.

**Additions:**
1. Add row to Test Suite Inventory table: Suite concurrent-cr-conflicts, CR Names keystone-concurrent-a + keystone-concurrent-b, Behavior validated: concurrent CRs with shared secretRef, sub-resource isolation, deletion cleanup.
2. Add detailed section under Test Suite Details with: purpose, steps table (6 steps), fixtures list, design notes about secretToKeystoneMapper behavior.
3. Update the ASCII overview diagram to include concurrent-cr-conflicts box.
4. Add to File Layout section: tests/e2e/keystone/concurrent-cr-conflicts/ with 3 files.

**Follow existing doc patterns** from basic-deployment and deletion-cleanup sections. Reference CC-0066 feature ID.

tasks.title: Update docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md with operator-pod-kill scenario documentation (REQ-008)
tasks.description:
Add the operator-pod-kill test suite to the existing Chaos E2E test reference documentation.

**Additions:**
1. Add row to Test Suite Inventory table: Suite operator-pod-kill, Scenario ID SC-CHAOS-004, CR Name keystone-chaos-op, Test Pattern operator-failover, Condition Assertions: Ready=True maintained → replica patch applied.
2. Add detailed section under Test Suite Details with: purpose, steps table (7 steps), fixtures list, PodChaos targeting details (mode: all, default namespace), design notes about leader election.
3. Add new Test Pattern section: Operator Failover (SC-CHAOS-004) describing the baseline → kill → recover → verify-reconciliation pattern.
4. Update Keystone CR Fixtures table: add keystone-chaos-op / keystone_chaos_op row.
5. Update Catch Block Diagnostics matrix table: add operator-pod-kill column.
6. Update File Layout section: add operator-pod-kill/ directory with 4 files.
7. Update Overview section count from '3 Phase 1 test suites' to '4 test suites' and add operator-pod-kill box to ASCII diagram.

**Follow existing doc patterns** from mariadb-pod-kill section. Reference CC-0066 feature ID.

review_criteria: Both concurrent CRs (keystone-concurrent-a, keystone-concurrent-b) reach Ready=True in E2E environment — run make e2e with tests/e2e/keystone/concurrent-cr-conflicts/
Sub-resource isolation verified: unique Deployment, Service, CronJob, ConfigMap per CR; no shared sub-resources
CR deletion isolation verified: deleting CR-A leaves CR-B Ready=True with all resources intact
Operator pod kill and recovery verified: PodChaos kills operator → new leader takes over → Ready=True maintained — run make e2e-chaos with tests/e2e-chaos/operator-pod-kill/
Post-failover reconciliation verified: replica patch applied by new leader after recovery
Every assert step in both test suites has a catch block with diagnostic output (CR status, pod logs, events)
All YAML files include SPDX-FileCopyrightText and SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 headers
All files reference CC-0066 feature ID in comments
CR fixture names and database names are unique across all test suites (no conflicts with basic-deployment, chaos-db, etc.)
Documentation updated: docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md and docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md reflect new test suites
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

This feature adds two E2E test suites with no production code changes:

```
tests/e2e/keystone/concurrent-cr-conflicts/    (happy-path, runs in e2e-operator CI job)
├── 00-keystone-cr-a.yaml
├── 01-keystone-cr-b.yaml
└── chainsaw-test.yaml

tests/e2e-chaos/operator-pod-kill/              (chaos, runs in e2e-chaos CI job)
├── 00-keystone-cr.yaml
├── 01-podchaos.yaml
├── 02-patch-replicas.yaml
└── chainsaw-test.yaml
```

## Data Flow: Concurrent CR Test

```
Apply CR-A + CR-B (shared secretRef: keystone-db, keystone-admin)
  │
  ├─ secretToKeystoneMapper enqueues BOTH CRs on shared Secret change
  │     (keystone_controller.go:211-233)
  │
  ├─ Reconciler for CR-A creates: keystone-concurrent-a-api (Deployment),
  │   keystone-concurrent-a-api (Service), keystone-concurrent-a-fernet-rotate
  │   (CronJob), keystone-concurrent-a-config-{hash} (ConfigMap),
  │   keystone-concurrent-a (Database/User/Grant)
  │
  ├─ Reconciler for CR-B creates: keystone-concurrent-b-api (Deployment),
  │   keystone-concurrent-b-api (Service), etc. — all uniquely named
  │
  ├─ Both CRs reach Ready=True independently
  │
  └─ Delete CR-A → owner ref GC cleans CR-A resources → CR-B unaffected
```

## Data Flow: Operator Pod Kill Test

```
Baseline: Keystone CR Ready=True, operator running 2 replicas
  │
  ├─ PodChaos mode:all kills BOTH operator pods
  │     (targets default namespace, label: app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone-operator)
  │
  ├─ Deployment controller creates 2 new pods
  │
  ├─ New pods start → compete for leader lease (keystone.openstack.c5c3.io)
  │
  ├─ Winner: starts controller manager → re-registers watches → processes queue
  │
  ├─ Keystone CR re-evaluated → all conditions still True → Ready=True
  │
  └─ Patch replicas 1→2 → new leader reconciles → Deployment scaled → Ready=True
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **mode: all for PodChaos**: Guarantees the leader is killed (not just the standby). Forces actual leader re-election.

2. **Shared secrets, different database names**: Both CRs share secretRef (keystone-db) and adminPasswordSecretRef (keystone-admin) to test mapper behavior, but use different database.database names (keystone_concurrent_a, keystone_concurrent_b) to avoid MariaDB-level conflicts.

3. **replicas: 1 for all test CRs**: Minimizes resource usage in CI kind cluster while still testing core reconciliation.

4. **Operator namespace targeting**: The operator is deployed to the default namespace via `hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh` (no -n flag). PodChaos targets `default` namespace. If deployment namespace changes, update 01-podchaos.yaml selector.namespaces.

5. **No CI workflow changes needed**: The concurrent-cr-conflicts test is picked up automatically by the e2e-operator job (tests/e2e/keystone/ path). The operator-pod-kill test is picked up by the e2e-chaos job (tests/e2e-chaos/ path).

## Potential Pitfalls

- **Operator namespace**: If the operator is deployed to a namespace other than `default`, the PodChaos selector.namespaces must be updated. The implementation should verify this.
- **Leader election timeout**: The default lease duration is 15s with renewal every 10s. After killing all pods, the new leader may take up to 15s + pod startup time to acquire the lease. The 5m assert timeout is generous.
- **Resource contention in CI**: The concurrent CR test creates 2 full Keystone stacks (2 Deployments, 2 db_sync Jobs, 2 bootstrap Jobs). CI kind cluster must have sufficient resources.
- **Database name uniqueness**: Each test suite uses unique database names (keystone_concurrent_a, keystone_concurrent_b, keystone_chaos_op) to avoid conflicts with existing tests.

## Deferred Items

1. **Concurrent CRs with same database name**: Testing two CRs with identical database.database values could expose MariaDB-level conflicts. Deferred because it may require production code changes to handle gracefully. Revisit after validating the basic shared-secret scenario.

2. **Kill operator during initial reconciliation**: The current test kills the operator after Ready=True baseline. Killing during initial reconciliation (before Ready) would test partial-status recovery. Deferred because reliable timing in Chainsaw is impractical. Could be tested via envtest integration test instead.

3. **envtest integration test for secretToKeystoneMapper with concurrent CRs**: A unit/integration test verifying the mapper returns correct reconcile.Request sets for multiple CRs. Deferred as a separate enhancement.

4. **Webhook availability during operator failover**: Testing that admission webhooks remain available (or gracefully fail) during operator pod kill. Deferred as a separate scope.

5. **Continuous chaos (duration-based pod kill)**: Testing with continuous PodChaos (duration: 60s) rather than one-shot. Deferred as a more advanced chaos scenario.
